task: GroundTruth Grants — sample curated pack (living-bible subscription proof)
disclaimer: Grant intelligence from public sources — not application or legal advice. Deadlines change; verify on cited portal before submission.
policy: Public .gov sources; Grants.gov API PARKED (403) — curated from opportunity detail pages and agency solicitations.

subscription_refresh.product_angle: Deadlines + new postings = recurring subscription value

prerequisites.title: SAM.gov entity registration + Unique Entity ID
prerequisites.summary: Organizations applying for federal awards as prime awardees must register in SAM.gov; registration enables bidding and federal assistance applications. Registration must be renewed every 365 days.

cited_facts.value: To apply for federal awards as a prime awardee, an organization needs a SAM.gov registration; registration assigns a Unique Entity ID and allows bidding on contracts and applying for federal assistance.
SAM.gov registration can take up to 10 business days to become active; entities must renew registration every 365 days to keep it active.

prerequisites.title: Grants.gov registration + Workspace
prerequisites.summary: Federal grant applicants search opportunities on Grants.gov, confirm eligibility, register, and submit via Workspace.

cited_facts.value: Grants.gov application workflow: Learn → Check eligibility → Search opportunities → Register → Apply via Workspace → Track submission status.

citation.source: Grants.gov — How to Apply for Grants

cited_facts.value: Small business federal grants may be awarded to companies meeting SBA size standards; most Grants.gov opportunities are for organizations, not individuals — personal financial assistance routes to USA.gov.

programs.title: NSF SBIR/STTR Phase I, Phase II, Fast-Track (NSF 26-510)
programs.sponsor_agency: U.S. National Science Foundation — Directorate for Technology, Innovation and Partnerships
requirements: Small business concern meeting SBA size standards (≤500 employees including affiliates)
Phase I: official invitation required after Project Pitch approval
Phase II: only prior NSF Phase I awardees; submit 6–24 months after Phase I start
STTR: requires partner research institution subaward

cited_facts.value: NSF 26-510 posted May 22, 2026; full proposal deadlines include July 27, 2026, November 4, 2026, and recurring first-Wednesday-in-November / first-Thursday-in-March / first-Wednesday-in-July cycles.
Phase I awards up to $305,000 (6–18 months); Phase II up to $1,250,000 (typically 24 months); Fast-Track up to $1,555,555; anticipated program budget $210,000,000 across SBIR/STTR award types.
America's Seed Fund (NSF SBIR/STTR) awards $200+ million annually to ~400 startups; up to $2M non-dilutive R&D with 0% equity — awardees retain full company and IP ownership.

citation.source: NSF Seed Fund — About the Program

gaps.label: Entity-specific Project Pitch invitation status
gaps.note: Invitation is per-company — requires applicant action; not in public index

programs.title: SBA Federal and State Technology (FAST) Partnership Program FY2026
requirements: Public or private entity endorsed by state governor as sole state applicant
Provide SBIR/STTR outreach, technical/business assistance, or financial support to small businesses
FY2026 FAST limited to specific states/territories per NOFO (e.g., CT, DC, MD, MA, NV, OR, SC, WA, VT, territories listed in source)
Cost sharing or matching required per opportunity listing

cited_facts.value: FAST FY2026 (SB-OIIFT-26-001) closes July 22, 2026; $9,000,000 program funding; 7 expected awards; award range $1–$180,000; posted June 2, 2026; archives August 15, 2026.

FAST mission: strengthen technological competitiveness of U.S. small businesses via state/regional programs raising SBIR/STTR awareness, educating on requirements, and increasing application submissions and awards.

gaps.label: Pennsylvania FAST applicant eligibility (PA not in FY2026 eligible-state list)
gaps.source_checked: FAST FY2026 NOFO eligible states list — https://simpler.grants.gov/opportunity/9ea5fbca-f285-4ad8-a14e-cc2c0abc0266
gaps.note: PA small businesses benefit indirectly via FAST-funded state programs in eligible states — not as FAST grantee

programs.title: SBIR/STTR Federal Program Hub
programs.sponsor_agency: U.S. Small Business Administration (SBIR/STTR policy) + participating agencies

deadlines.label: Agency-specific solicitations
deadlines.note: Each participating agency publishes its own NOFO cycles — monitor via SBIR.gov and Grants.gov search

requirements: U.S. small business concern per SBA size standards
Work performed in United States (with limited exceptions per SBIR/STTR Policy Directive)

cited_facts.value: SBIR.gov is the federal SBIR/STTR program hub; eligibility size/compliance guidance published by SBA (site root accessible; legacy /about-sbir path returns 404).

gaps.label: Consolidated live deadline index for all 11 SBIR agencies
gaps.source_checked: https://www.sbir.gov/ — no single machine-readable deadline rollup at harvest
gaps.note: Living-bible refresh harvests per-agency solicitations (NIH, DOE, USDA, etc.) into vertical pack

programs.title: Pennsylvania DCED Programs & Funding (state layer)
programs.sponsor_agency: PA Department of Community & Economic Development
requirements: Varies by program — DCED publishes separate NOFAs/RFPs per initiative

cited_facts.value: PA DCED Programs & Funding portal indexes state economic development grants, tax credits, and business assistance programs for Pennsylvania entities.

gaps.label: Unified PA state grant deadline calendar with award amounts
gaps.note: State pack expansion required — per-program harvest into states/pa/programs.json

cross_cutting_gaps.note: PARKED — use simpler.grants.gov + search-results-detail pages until API access restored
